# Ralph Progress ŌĆö LAN & Bluetooth iteration
# Auto-updated by ralph.sh ŌĆö do not edit manually

## Codebase Patterns
- Auth is opt-in: `AUTH_ENABLED` env var (default false). `/health` endpoint exposes `"auth": true|false` for client discovery.
- macOS client uses `setAuthHeader(&request)` helper to conditionally add Bearer token. Windows uses `SetAuthHeader(client)` overload.
- `isConfigured` only requires `hostURL`/`HostUrl` ŌĆö token is optional for LAN-only deployments.
- Branch `ralph/lan-and-bluetooth` is based on `ralph/copy-everywhere-queue-ux` (not `main`), since `main` only has 2 initial commits.
- mDNS service type is `_copyeverywhere._tcp` (constant in `discovery.ServiceType`). TXT records: `version`, `auth`. Clients browse this same type for auto-discovery.
- Server host app (`macos/CopyEverywhereServer/`) follows the same AppDelegate + NSStatusItem + NSPopover pattern as the client. `ServerProcess` is the subprocess manager (`@MainActor` ObservableObject).
- Go binary path convention: sibling `copyeverywhere-server` next to the Swift executable. Build independently with `go build -o copyeverywhere-server .` in `server/`.
- Server host app config persisted to `~/Library/Application Support/CopyEverywhereServer/config.json`. `ServerConfig.environment` provides the env dict for `ServerProcess`.
- Launch at Login uses `SMAppService.mainApp` (ServiceManagement, macOS 13+). Sync actual status on init via `.status == .enabled` since users can toggle login items in System Settings independently.
- Adding a new server env var requires updates in 5 places: config.Config + Load(), main.go usage, docker-compose.yml, ServerConfig.swift (env dict + persistence + ConfigData), MenuBarView.swift (config UI).
- macOS client uses `NWBrowser` (Network.framework) for Bonjour discovery of `_copyeverywhere._tcp` services. Resolve endpoints via temporary `NWConnection` to get IP:port.
- Windows client uses `Zeroconf` NuGet for mDNS browsing. Service type is `_copyeverywhere._tcp.local.` (note trailing `.local.`). Periodic scan via `ZeroconfResolver.ResolveAsync`.
- `ServerAuthRequired` property (nullable bool) on ConfigStore is populated from mDNS TXT `auth` field or `/health` response `auth` field. Controls Access Token field visibility.
- CopyEverywhere Bluetooth RFCOMM UUID: `CE000001-1000-1000-8000-00805F9B34FB`. Defined as `kCopyEverywhereServiceUUID` (macOS IOBluetoothSDPUUID). Same UUID must be used on Windows.
- `BluetoothService` (`@MainActor`) is the RFCOMM connection layer. Server mode: SDP publish + channel notification. Client mode: SDP query + RFCOMM open. IOBluetooth callbacks arrive on non-main threads ŌĆö use `nonisolated` bridge methods + `Task { @MainActor in }`.
- IOBluetooth API: use `hasService(from:)` not the obsoleted `hasServiceFromArray(_:)`. `IOBluetoothSDPUUID(bytes:length:)` returns non-optional.
- Bluetooth RFCOMM protocol: newline-delimited JSON headers (`0x0A`) + raw content bytes. Handshake: `{"app":"CopyEverywhere","version":"3.0"}\n`. Transfer: `BluetoothTransferHeader` JSON + `\n` + content.
- `BluetoothSession` wraps `IOBluetoothRFCOMMChannel` as its delegate. Buffer-based state machine: handshake ŌåÆ header ŌåÆ content. `BluetoothService` owns `activeSession`.
- IOBluetooth `closeChannel()` is obsoleted in Swift 3 ŌĆö use `channel.close()` instead (returns IOReturn).
- `IOBluetoothDeviceInquiry(delegate:)` returns optional. SDP filtering of discovered devices requires per-device helper objects retained until query completes.
- `TransferMode` enum (`.lanServer`/`.bluetooth`) persisted in UserDefaults. Paired devices persisted as JSON-encoded `[PairedBluetoothDevice]` in UserDefaults.
- All send entry points route through `transferMode` at the top of `sendClipboardText`/`sendText`/`sendFile`. `isSendReady` is the universal readiness check.
- `BluetoothSession.sendText`/`sendFile` return `AsyncStream<Double>` for progress. Use `withCheckedContinuation` to bridge to async/await.
- RFCOMM server must be started explicitly in Bluetooth mode (`startBluetoothServerIfNeeded`). Both `autoReconnectBluetooth()` and `setTransferMode(.bluetooth)` handle this.
- Bluetooth event delegation chain: `BluetoothSession` ŌåÆ `BluetoothService` (as `BluetoothSessionDelegate`) ŌåÆ `ConfigStore` (as `BluetoothServiceDelegate`). All new events need wiring through both layers.
- Windows Bluetooth RFCOMM uses WinRT APIs. TFM must be `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0` (not plain `net8.0-windows`) for `Windows.Devices.Bluetooth.Rfcomm` access.
- Windows `BluetoothService` uses events (`ConnectionAccepted`, `Connected`, `ConnectionFailed`) instead of macOS delegate pattern. `StreamSocket` + `DataWriter`/`DataReader` are the I/O primitives.
- Windows `BluetoothSession` mirrors macOS `BluetoothSession` ŌĆö same wire format, handshake, transfer protocol. Uses events instead of delegate pattern. `BluetoothService.CreateSession()` auto-creates on connect.
- For cross-platform JSON enum interop (macOS sends lowercase), use string-backed properties in C# instead of `JsonStringEnumConverter`.
- Windows `ConfigStore` persists Bluetooth config (`TransferMode`, `PairedDevices`) in the same `config.json` as device config. Use `AddPairedDevice()`/`RemovePairedDevice()` helpers ŌĆö they auto-persist.
- Windows Bluetooth UI is dynamically generated (clearing/rebuilding StackPanel children) same as discovered servers list. `RenderPairedDevices()`/`RenderDiscoveredBtDevices()` are the refresh methods.
- Windows `SendService` is the single send dispatcher for all UI entry points. It checks `ConfigStore.TransferMode` and routes to `BluetoothSession` or `ApiClient`. Never bypass it for sends.
- Windows `ConfigStore.IsSendReady` is the universal readiness check for send guards ŌĆö true when LAN configured or Bluetooth connected. Use instead of `IsConfigured` in send paths.
- Windows RFCOMM server must be explicitly started via `StartBluetoothServerIfNeeded()` for the device to accept inbound Bluetooth connections. Call from init and mode switch.
- Windows `BluetoothService.CreateSession()` is the single place to wire all session events (handshake, transfer, progress, failure). Add new events there.

## 2026-04-12 - US-035
- What was implemented:
  - Added `AUTH_ENABLED` env var (default false) to server config
  - `getEnvBool()` helper in config/config.go
  - main.go conditionally applies auth middleware only when `AUTH_ENABLED=true`
  - `/health` endpoint now returns `"auth": true|false`
  - macOS: `setAuthHeader(&request)` helper skips header when token is empty; `isConfigured` no longer requires token
  - macOS: ConfigView shows token as optional with updated placeholder
  - Windows: `SetAuthHeader(HttpClient)` overload conditionally sets header; `IsConfigured` no longer requires token
  - Windows: MainWindow save flow no longer blocks on empty token; SSE client conditionally sets auth
  - docker-compose.yml updated with `AUTH_ENABLED` env var
  - New middleware/auth_test.go with 5 test cases
- Files changed:
  - server/config/config.go ŌĆö added AuthEnabled field, getEnvBool helper
  - server/main.go ŌĆö conditional auth middleware, auth field in /health
  - server/middleware/auth_test.go ŌĆö new test file
  - server/docker-compose.yml ŌĆö AUTH_ENABLED env var
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö setAuthHeader helper, isConfigured relaxed
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigView.swift ŌĆö token optional, save/test enabled without token
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ConfigStore.cs ŌĆö IsConfigured relaxed
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ApiClient.cs ŌĆö SetAuthHeader(HttpClient) overload, conditional auth
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö removed token requirement for save, conditional SSE auth
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö updated commands and conventions
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The main branch only has initial setup commits; all code lives on `ralph/copy-everywhere-queue-ux`. New feature branches should fork from there.
  - macOS ConfigStore has ~15 places setting the auth header ŌĆö centralizing into a helper was essential.
  - Windows ApiClient already had a `SetAuthHeader()` but only for the default `_httpClient`. An overload accepting `HttpClient` was needed for the one-off clients (download, upload).
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-036
- What was implemented:
  - New `discovery/` package wrapping `github.com/hashicorp/mdns` (pure-Go, no CGO)
  - `discovery.Start(port, version, authEnabled)` registers mDNS service type `_copyeverywhere._tcp` with TXT records `version=<v>` and `auth=<bool>`
  - `MDNSServer.Shutdown()` deregisters the service (idempotent)
  - `main.go` starts mDNS after config load; signal handler deregisters on SIGINT/SIGTERM
  - Tests verify Start/Shutdown lifecycle and idempotent shutdown; discovery test is non-fatal (multicast depends on network environment)
- Files changed:
  - server/discovery/mdns.go ŌĆö new mDNS service advertisement package
  - server/discovery/mdns_test.go ŌĆö lifecycle and discovery tests
  - server/main.go ŌĆö mDNS startup + signal-based shutdown
  - server/go.mod, server/go.sum ŌĆö added github.com/hashicorp/mdns dependency
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented mDNS pattern
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-036 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `github.com/hashicorp/mdns` is the go-to pure-Go mDNS library. It wraps `github.com/miekg/dns` underneath.
  - mDNS multicast discovery is unreliable in isolated/CI environments ŌĆö keep discovery tests non-fatal or skip in short mode.
  - Service type `_copyeverywhere._tcp` is the shared constant in `discovery.ServiceType` ŌĆö clients should use the same string for browsing.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-037A
- What was implemented:
  - New `macos/CopyEverywhereServer/` SPM project (macOS 13+, executable target)
  - `CopyEverywhereServerApp.swift` ŌĆö @main entry point + AppDelegate with NSStatusItem + NSPopover (same pattern as client)
  - `ServerProcess.swift` ŌĆö @MainActor ObservableObject managing Go server binary via Foundation.Process
    - start/stop/restart lifecycle; SIGTERM for graceful shutdown
    - stdout/stderr captured via Pipe with readabilityHandler, surfaced as @Published logLines
    - terminationHandler detects unexpected exits
  - `MenuBarView.swift` ŌĆö SwiftUI panel with status badge (green/gray), Start/Stop/Restart buttons, scrollable log viewer, Quit button
  - Menu bar icon tints green when running, gray when stopped
  - NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory) hides Dock icon
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Package.swift ŌĆö new SPM manifest
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/.gitignore ŌĆö .build/, .swiftpm/
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/CopyEverywhereServerApp.swift ŌĆö app + delegate
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/ServerProcess.swift ŌĆö subprocess manager
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/MenuBarView.swift ŌĆö panel UI
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö architecture and conventions for server host app
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-037A passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `Process.terminate()` sends SIGTERM on macOS ŌĆö matches Go server's existing signal handler for mDNS deregistration.
  - `Pipe.fileHandleForReading.readabilityHandler` is the async-compatible way to stream subprocess output without blocking.
  - Icon tinting via `NSImage` drawing with `.sourceAtop` compositing works for SF Symbols but requires `isTemplate = false`.
  - The server host app is intentionally minimal for US-037A ŌĆö config panel (US-037B) and launch-at-login (US-037C) are separate stories.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-037B
- What was implemented:
  - New `ServerConfig.swift` ŌĆö `@MainActor` ObservableObject persisting config to `~/Library/Application Support/CopyEverywhereServer/config.json`
  - Config fields: port (default 8080), storage path (default ~/Library/Application Support/CopyEverywhereServer/data), TTL hours, auth enabled, access token
  - `ServerConfig.environment` computed property provides env vars for the Go subprocess
  - `ServerProcess.config` now points to `ServerConfig` instead of a raw `[String: String]` dict
  - `MenuBarView` expanded with: status section (listen address, storage path, used space), config fields with Save/Save & Restart, Clear logs button, Browse button for storage path
  - `AppDelegate` creates both `ServerConfig` and `ServerProcess`, wires them together, passes both as `@EnvironmentObject`
  - Popover size increased to 520pt to fit new config section
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/ServerConfig.swift ŌĆö new file
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/MenuBarView.swift ŌĆö rewritten with config panel
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/ServerProcess.swift ŌĆö config property replaces environment dict
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/CopyEverywhereServerApp.swift ŌĆö wires ServerConfig
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented ServerConfig persistence pattern
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-037B passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `@MainActor` classes can't call their own static methods from `Task.detached` ŌĆö use `nonisolated static` for pure utility functions like directory size calculation.
  - SwiftUI `frame(width:maxHeight:)` doesn't exist as a combined overload ŌĆö use `frame(width:height:)` with a `ScrollView` inside for flexible content.
  - `ServerConfig` default storage path uses Application Support, not the working directory, since the SPM executable doesn't have a stable working dir.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-037C
- What was implemented:
  - Added `launchAtLogin` property to `ServerConfig` with persistence in config.json
  - Uses `SMAppService.mainApp` (ServiceManagement framework, macOS 13+) to register/unregister launch at login
  - `didSet` on the property calls register/unregister immediately when toggled
  - `syncLaunchAtLoginStatus()` on init reads the actual SMAppService status to sync the published property
  - Added "Launch at Login" toggle to MenuBarView config section
  - `ConfigData` uses `launchAtLogin: Bool?` (optional) for backward-compatible deserialization of existing config files
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/ServerConfig.swift ŌĆö launchAtLogin property, SMAppService register/unregister, sync on init
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/MenuBarView.swift ŌĆö added Launch at Login toggle
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-037C passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `SMAppService.mainApp` requires a proper app bundle with a bundle identifier to work at runtime. SPM executables lack this by default ŌĆö the feature will build but won't function until the app is packaged as a .app bundle.
  - Use `SMAppService.mainApp.status == .enabled` to read current state on launch rather than relying solely on persisted config, since the user can change login items in System Settings independently.
  - Making `ConfigData.launchAtLogin` optional (`Bool?`) avoids breaking deserialization of existing config files that don't have the field.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-038
- What was implemented:
  - Added `BIND_ADDRESS` env var (default `0.0.0.0`) to server `config.Config`
  - `main.go` listen address changed from `:<port>` to `<bind_address>:<port>`; startup log prints actual listen address
  - MenuBarExtra `ServerConfig.swift`: added `bindAddress` property, persisted in config.json, forwarded via `environment` dict
  - MenuBarExtra `MenuBarView.swift`: added "Bind address" config field; status section shows dynamic bind address instead of hardcoded `0.0.0.0`
  - `docker-compose.yml` updated with `BIND_ADDRESS` env var
  - CLAUDE.md updated with BIND_ADDRESS convention
- Files changed:
  - server/config/config.go ŌĆö added BindAddress field
  - server/main.go ŌĆö use BindAddress in listen addr format
  - server/docker-compose.yml ŌĆö BIND_ADDRESS env var
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/ServerConfig.swift ŌĆö bindAddress property + persistence
  - macos/CopyEverywhereServer/Sources/CopyEverywhereServer/MenuBarView.swift ŌĆö bind address config field + dynamic status
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented BIND_ADDRESS convention
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-038 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - When adding new env vars to the Go server, remember to also update: (1) `config.Config` struct + `Load()`, (2) `main.go` usage, (3) `docker-compose.yml`, (4) `ServerConfig.swift` environment dict + persistence, (5) `MenuBarView.swift` config UI.
  - `ConfigData` fields for new optional settings should be `Optional` (`String?`, `Bool?`) to maintain backward compatibility with existing config.json files.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-039
- What was implemented:
  - New `BonjourBrowser.swift` ŌĆö `@MainActor` ObservableObject using `NWBrowser` (Network.framework) to browse `_copyeverywhere._tcp` services
  - `DiscoveredServer` model with id, name, host, port, authRequired, version fields
  - NWBrowser resolves endpoints via NWConnection to get actual IP:port; TXT records parsed for `auth` and `version`
  - `ConfigStore` owns `BonjourBrowser` instance, starts browsing on init
  - `selectDiscoveredServer(_:)` sets hostURL and serverAuthRequired from the discovered server
  - `serverAuthRequired` published property: populated from mDNS TXT record or `/health` response `auth` field
  - `testConnection()` now reads `auth` from `/health` JSON response to update `serverAuthRequired`
  - `ConfigView` updated with "Discovered Servers" section showing LAN servers with click-to-select
  - Selected server highlighted with checkmark and blue background
  - Server entries show name, host:port, version badge, and lock icon for auth-required servers
  - Access Token field hidden when `serverAuthRequired == false`; shown as required when `true`, optional when unknown
  - Manual Host URL input preserved as fallback below discovered servers
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BonjourBrowser.swift ŌĆö new file
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö bonjourBrowser, serverAuthRequired, selectDiscoveredServer, /health auth parsing
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigView.swift ŌĆö discovered servers UI, conditional token field
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-039 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `NWBrowser` (Network.framework) is the modern API for Bonjour browsing on macOS 13+. No need for `NetServiceBrowser`.
  - `NWTXTRecord.getEntry(for:)` returns `NWTXTRecord.Entry?` enum ŌĆö use a custom `stringValue(for:)` wrapper to extract string values.
  - `NWTXTRecord.Entry` cases are `.string`, `.data`, `.empty`, `.none` ŌĆö no `.rawBytes` case exists.
  - To resolve a Bonjour service endpoint to an actual IP:port, create a temporary `NWConnection` and read `currentPath?.remoteEndpoint` when `.ready`.
  - `NWBrowser.browseResultsChangedHandler` fires with the full current set of results, not deltas ŌĆö diff against existing state manually.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-040
- What was implemented:
  - Added `Zeroconf` NuGet package (v3.6.11) for .NET mDNS/DNS-SD browsing
  - New `Services/MdnsDiscoveryService.cs` ŌĆö periodic mDNS scan for `_copyeverywhere._tcp` services using `ZeroconfResolver.ResolveAsync`
  - `DiscoveredServer` model with Id, Name, Host, Port, AuthRequired, Version fields (mirrors macOS `DiscoveredServer` struct)
  - `ConfigStore.cs` ŌĆö added `ServerAuthRequired` property (nullable bool: null=unknown, true/false from mDNS TXT or /health)
  - `ApiClient.cs` ŌĆö `HealthResponse` now parses `auth` field from `/health`; `TestConnectionAsync` updates `ConfigStore.ServerAuthRequired`
  - `MainWindow.xaml` ŌĆö added "Discovered Servers" section with scrollable list above manual Host URL input; Access Token wrapped in conditional `AccessTokenPanel`
  - `MainWindow.xaml.cs` ŌĆö `MdnsDiscoveryService` instantiated and started in constructor; `OnDiscoveredServersChanged` dynamically builds server list with click-to-select; `SelectDiscoveredServer` sets HostUrl and auth from mDNS; `UpdateAccessTokenVisibility` hides token field when auth=false
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/CopyEverywhere.csproj ŌĆö added Zeroconf NuGet
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/MdnsDiscoveryService.cs ŌĆö new file
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ConfigStore.cs ŌĆö ServerAuthRequired property
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ApiClient.cs ŌĆö HealthResponse.Auth, TestConnectionAsync updates auth
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml ŌĆö Discovered Servers UI, conditional Access Token panel
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö discovery wiring, server selection, auth visibility
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-040 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `Zeroconf` NuGet is the simplest .NET mDNS browsing library. `ZeroconfResolver.ResolveAsync(serviceType, scanTime)` does a one-shot scan; wrap in a loop for continuous discovery.
  - Zeroconf service type must end with `.local.` (e.g., `_copyeverywhere._tcp.local.`).
  - TXT record properties in Zeroconf are `IReadOnlyList<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>>` ŌĆö iterate the outer list and check each dict.
  - WPF dynamically generated UI (clearing StackPanel children) works well for discovered server lists; avoid `x:Name` on children that will be removed.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö note this in progress when Windows-only changes are made.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-041A
- What was implemented:
  - New `BluetoothService.swift` ŌĆö `@MainActor` ObservableObject wrapping IOBluetooth framework for RFCOMM connections
  - Defines shared RFCOMM Service UUID `CE000001-1000-1000-8000-00805F9B34FB` as `kCopyEverywhereServiceUUID`
  - Server mode: publishes SDP service record via `IOBluetoothSDPServiceRecord.publishedServiceRecord(with:)`, registers for inbound RFCOMM channel notifications
  - Client mode: `SDPQueryHelper` performs async SDP query on remote device, finds CopyEverywhere service record, opens RFCOMM channel
  - `BluetoothServiceDelegate` protocol for connection callbacks (didAcceptConnection, didConnectTo, didFailWithError)
  - `BluetoothServiceError` enum for all failure cases (serviceUUIDNotFound, sdpQueryFailed, rfcommOpenFailed, etc.)
  - IOBluetooth callbacks bridge to MainActor via `nonisolated` methods + `Task { @MainActor in }`
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothService.swift ŌĆö new file
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-041A passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - IOBluetooth SDP query callbacks arrive on non-main threads. Since `BluetoothService` is `@MainActor`, bridge methods must be `nonisolated` and dispatch back via `Task { @MainActor in }`.
  - `IOBluetoothSDPUUID(bytes:length:)` returns a non-optional ŌĆö no force unwrapping needed.
  - Use `hasService(from:)` not `hasServiceFromArray(_:)` ŌĆö the latter is obsoleted in Swift 3.
  - SDP service record dict keys use the format `"0001 - ServiceClassIDList"`, `"0004 - ProtocolDescriptorList"`, `"0100 - ServiceName*"`.
  - `publishedServiceRecord(with:)` takes `[AnyHashable: Any]` ŌĆö cast array values explicitly with `as [Any]` to satisfy the type checker.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-041B
- What was implemented:
  - New `BluetoothProtocol.swift` ŌĆö app-layer protocol on top of RFCOMM connections
  - `BluetoothSession` class wrapping `IOBluetoothRFCOMMChannel` as its delegate
  - Handshake: both sides exchange `{"app":"CopyEverywhere","version":"3.0"}\n` with 5s timeout
  - Transfer protocol: `BluetoothTransferHeader` JSON (`type`, `filename`, `size`) + newline delimiter + raw content bytes
  - `sendText(_:)` for text payloads, `sendFile(url:)` with FileHandle chunk-reads for file payloads (16KB RFCOMM chunks)
  - Receiver uses buffer-based state machine: handshake phase ŌåÆ header parse ŌåÆ content accumulation
  - `BluetoothSessionDelegate` protocol for handshake/transfer callbacks
  - `BluetoothService` extended: `activeSession` property, `createSession(channel:device:)` auto-starts handshake on connect
  - `BluetoothService` conforms to `BluetoothSessionDelegate` and forwards events to `BluetoothServiceDelegate`
  - `BluetoothServiceDelegate` extended with `sessionReady` and `sessionHandshakeFailed` callbacks
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothProtocol.swift ŌĆö new file
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothService.swift ŌĆö session management + delegate conformance
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Bluetooth protocol conventions
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-041B passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - IOBluetooth `closeChannel()` is obsoleted in Swift 3 ŌĆö use `channel.close()` instead (returns IOReturn, discard with `_ =`).
  - RFCOMM data arrives in `rfcommChannelData` on a non-main thread. Buffer in `Data` and process on `@MainActor` via `Task`.
  - Newline (`0x0A`) is a clean delimiter for JSON headers over RFCOMM since JSON itself doesn't contain raw newlines.
  - `writeAsync` on `IOBluetoothRFCOMMChannel` takes `UnsafeMutableRawPointer` ŌĆö use `NSMutableData.mutableBytes` to bridge from Swift `Data`.
  - File sends must chunk-read via `FileHandle` (16KB per RFCOMM write) to avoid OOM on large files ŌĆö same pattern as the LAN chunked uploader.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-042
- What was implemented:
  - New `BluetoothDiscovery.swift` ŌĆö `@MainActor` ObservableObject wrapping `IOBluetoothDeviceInquiry` for scanning nearby Bluetooth devices
  - `DiscoveredBluetoothDevice` model + `PairedBluetoothDevice` (Codable) for persistence
  - SDP filter: each discovered device is queried for CopyEverywhere RFCOMM UUID before appearing in scan results
  - `BluetoothPairHelper` class manages system-level Bluetooth pairing flow via `device.openConnection()`, then triggers RFCOMM connection + app handshake
  - `ConfigStore` extended with: `TransferMode` enum (`.lanServer`/`.bluetooth`), paired devices list (persisted to UserDefaults), `BluetoothConnectionStatus`, `BluetoothServiceDelegate` conformance
  - `autoReconnectBluetooth()` method for reconnecting to first paired device on launch
  - `ConfigView` rewritten with segmented `Picker` for transfer mode toggle
  - LAN mode: existing server discovery, host URL, token, device registration UI
  - Bluetooth mode: connection status badge, paired devices list with Connect/Disconnect/Forget actions, scan button with SDP-filtered results, tap-to-pair flow
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothDiscovery.swift ŌĆö new file
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö TransferMode, BT properties, pairing/reconnect logic, BluetoothServiceDelegate
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigView.swift ŌĆö mode toggle + Bluetooth UI section
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented BluetoothDiscovery, BluetoothPairHelper, TransferMode patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-042 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `IOBluetoothDeviceInquiry(delegate:)` returns optional ŌĆö must `guard let`.
  - `IOBluetoothDeviceInquiryDelegate` methods arrive on non-main threads ŌĆö bridge with `nonisolated` + `Task { @MainActor in }`, same pattern as other IOBluetooth delegates.
  - SDP filtering of discovered devices requires a separate helper class (NSObject subclass with `sdpQueryComplete` callback) per device, retained in an array until the query completes.
  - `device.openConnection(self)` triggers the macOS system Bluetooth pairing dialog. If the device is already paired, skip straight to RFCOMM connection.
  - Paired device persistence uses simple JSON-encoded `[PairedBluetoothDevice]` in UserDefaults ŌĆö lightweight enough for a small list of devices.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-043
- What was implemented:
  - `BluetoothSession.sendText(_:)` and `sendFile(url:)` now return `AsyncStream<Double>` for send progress tracking
  - File send progress reports per-chunk (16KB RFCOMM chunks) via continuation yield
  - Three new private Bluetooth send methods in `ConfigStore`: `sendClipboardTextBluetooth()`, `sendTextBluetooth(_:)`, `sendFileBluetooth(url:)`
  - All three public send entry points (`sendClipboardText`, `sendText`, `sendFile`) route through `transferMode` at the top ŌĆö Bluetooth mode dispatches to private BT methods, LAN mode uses existing API calls
  - `isSendReady` computed property: returns true when LAN is configured (LAN mode) or Bluetooth is connected + handshake complete (BT mode)
  - AppDelegate drop handlers (`handleFileDrop`, `handleTextDrop`) now check `isSendReady` instead of `isConfigured`
  - Bluetooth send methods reuse existing `fileUploadProgress`, `fileUploadSpeed`, `fileUploadStatus`, and `sendStatus` published properties ŌĆö same progress UI works for both modes
  - Notifications: existing notification paths in AppDelegate (drop handlers, Cmd+V paste) work unchanged since they observe the same status properties
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothProtocol.swift ŌĆö sendText/sendFile return AsyncStream<Double>, progress tracking in streamFileContent
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö isSendReady, sendClipboardTextBluetooth, sendTextBluetooth, sendFileBluetooth, transferMode routing in sendClipboardText/sendText/sendFile
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/CopyEverywhereApp.swift ŌĆö drop handlers use isSendReady
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented send routing and Bluetooth progress patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-043 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `AsyncStream.makeStream()` is the cleanest way to create a continuation-based stream in Swift 5.9+. No need for the old `AsyncStream { continuation in }` init.
  - Bluetooth send methods use `withCheckedContinuation` to bridge completion-handler-based `BluetoothSession` APIs to async/await ŌĆö the progress stream runs concurrently in a separate `Task`.
  - Reusing existing `@Published` properties (`fileUploadProgress`, `sendStatus`) for Bluetooth sends avoids duplicating UI ŌĆö the same `MainPanelView` progress views work for both LAN and Bluetooth.
  - The `isSendReady` pattern (computed property checking mode-specific readiness) is the right abstraction for gating all send paths without duplicating mode checks everywhere.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-044
- What was implemented:
  - `BluetoothServiceDelegate` extended with `didReceive(payload:from:)`, `receiveProgress(_:header:)`, and `didFailReceivingWithError(_:)` callbacks
  - `BluetoothService` now forwards `BluetoothSessionDelegate` receive events to its own delegate (was previously stubbed out)
  - `BluetoothSession.processTransferData()` reports receive progress via `session(_:receiveProgress:header:)` as content bytes accumulate
  - Size verification: received `contentData.count` is checked against `header.size` ŌĆö mismatch triggers new `BluetoothSessionError.sizeMismatch` error
  - `ConfigStore` handles received payloads: text ŌåÆ NSPasteboard + notification, file ŌåÆ ~/Downloads (with dedup) + notification + Finder reveal
  - `bluetoothReceiveProgress` and `bluetoothReceiveFilename` published properties drive the receive progress UI
  - `MainPanelView` shows a blue "Receiving via Bluetooth" progress bar when `bluetoothReceiveProgress > 0`
  - RFCOMM server auto-starts in Bluetooth mode via `startBluetoothServerIfNeeded()` ŌĆö called from both `autoReconnectBluetooth()` and `setTransferMode(_:)`
  - `setTransferMode(_:)` stops the RFCOMM server when switching away from Bluetooth mode
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothProtocol.swift ŌĆö receiveProgress delegate method, sizeMismatch error, progress reporting in processTransferData
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/BluetoothService.swift ŌĆö receive delegate methods, receiveProgress forwarding
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö receive handlers, RFCOMM server lifecycle, receive progress properties
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/MainPanelView.swift ŌĆö Bluetooth receive progress UI
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Bluetooth receive and RFCOMM server auto-start patterns
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `BluetoothService` delegates receive events from `BluetoothSessionDelegate` to `BluetoothServiceDelegate` ŌĆö the double-delegate chain (Session ŌåÆ Service ŌåÆ ConfigStore) is the established pattern for all Bluetooth events.
  - Receive progress is calculated as `(totalSize - bytesRemaining + min(buffered, remaining)) / totalSize` ŌĆö this accounts for data already consumed from the buffer in previous calls.
  - RFCOMM server must be explicitly started for the device to accept inbound connections. Unlike LAN SSE (where the client connects to the server), Bluetooth mode requires both sides to publish SDP records and listen.
  - File dedup in ~/Downloads uses the same pattern as the LAN queue receive: `base (N).ext` incrementing counter.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-045A
- What was implemented:
  - New `Services/BluetoothService.cs` ŌĆö RFCOMM connection layer for Windows using WinRT APIs
  - Defines shared UUID `CE000001-1000-1000-8000-00805F9B34FB` as `BluetoothService.CopyEverywhereServiceUuid` (matches macOS)
  - Server mode: `RfcommServiceProvider.CreateAsync()` + `StreamSocketListener` for accepting inbound RFCOMM connections; SDP service name attribute written manually
  - Client mode: `RfcommDeviceService.FromIdAsync()` for connecting via `DeviceInformation`; `ConnectByAddressAsync()` for reconnecting to known paired devices via Bluetooth address
  - `FindDevicesAsync()` static helper using `RfcommDeviceService.GetDeviceSelector()` + `DeviceInformation.FindAllAsync()`
  - Active connection management: `StreamSocket`, `DataWriter`, `DataReader` with `InputStreamOptions.Partial`
  - Events: `ConnectionAccepted`, `Connected`, `ConnectionFailed` (mirrors macOS delegate pattern)
  - Implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` + `IDisposable`
  - Updated `.csproj` TFM from `net8.0-windows` to `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0` for WinRT API access
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/BluetoothService.cs ŌĆö new file
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/CopyEverywhere.csproj ŌĆö TFM updated to net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Windows Bluetooth RFCOMM patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-045A passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - WinRT Bluetooth APIs require TFM `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0` or later ŌĆö no extra NuGet needed, projections are auto-included.
  - `RfcommServiceProvider.SdpRawAttributes` requires manually writing SDP attribute bytes (type byte + length + value) ŌĆö there's no high-level helper.
  - `BluetoothDevice.FromHostNameAsync()` can resolve the remote device from a `StreamSocket` connection's `RemoteHostName`, but may fail for some connection types ŌĆö handle gracefully.
  - `ConnectByAddressAsync()` (via `BluetoothDevice.FromBluetoothAddressAsync` + `GetRfcommServicesForIdAsync`) is the pattern for reconnecting to known devices without re-scanning.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö Windows-only changes require manual verification on a Windows host.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-045B
- What was implemented:
  - New `Services/BluetoothSession.cs` ŌĆö app-layer protocol on top of RFCOMM StreamSocket, mirroring macOS `BluetoothProtocol.swift`
  - Handshake: both sides exchange `{"app":"CopyEverywhere","version":"3.0"}\n` with 5s timeout
  - Transfer protocol: `BluetoothTransferHeader` JSON (`type`, `filename`, `size`) + newline delimiter + raw content bytes
  - `SendTextAsync(text, progress)` for text payloads, `SendFileAsync(filePath, progress)` with chunked file streaming (16KB chunks)
  - Receive loop using `DataReader.LoadAsync()` with buffer-based state machine: handshake ŌåÆ header ŌåÆ content accumulation
  - Size verification: received byte count checked against header-declared size on completion
  - Events: `HandshakeCompleted`, `HandshakeFailed`, `TransferReceived`, `ReceiveProgress`, `ReceiveFailed`
  - Progress reporting via `IProgress<double>` for send, events for receive
  - `BluetoothService.cs` updated: `ActiveSession` property, `CreateSession()` auto-creates session on connect, `SessionReady`/`SessionHandshakeFailed` events, session disposed on disconnect
  - Cross-platform interop: identical wire format to macOS (JSON headers + newline delimiter + content bytes)
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/BluetoothSession.cs ŌĆö new file
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/BluetoothService.cs ŌĆö session management, events
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Windows Bluetooth protocol patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-045B passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `JsonStringEnumConverter` in .NET 8 uses PascalCase by default. For cross-platform interop with macOS (which sends lowercase `"text"`/`"file"`), use a string-backed property with a `[JsonIgnore]` convenience enum property.
  - `DataReader.LoadAsync()` with `InputStreamOptions.Partial` returns available bytes without blocking for a full buffer ŌĆö essential for streaming protocols.
  - `BluetoothSession` creates its own `DataWriter`/`DataReader` from the socket ŌĆö independent of `BluetoothService`'s writer/reader which remain for backward compatibility.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö Windows-only changes require manual verification on a Windows host.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-046
- What was implemented:
  - `ConfigStore.cs` extended with `TransferMode` enum (`LanServer`/`Bluetooth`), `PairedBluetoothDevice` model, `BluetoothConnectionStatus` enum
  - New properties: `TransferMode`, `PairedDevices`, `BluetoothConnectionStatus`, `BluetoothConnectedDeviceName`, `BluetoothErrorMessage`
  - `AddPairedDevice()`/`RemovePairedDevice()` helpers with auto-persistence to `config.json`
  - `DeviceConfig` extended with `transfer_mode` and `paired_devices` JSON fields
  - `MainWindow.xaml` ŌĆö Transfer Mode ComboBox (LAN Server / Bluetooth Direct), Bluetooth section with connection status badge, paired devices list, scan button, discovered devices list, scan progress/error display
  - `MainWindow.xaml.cs` ŌĆö full Bluetooth UI wiring: `BluetoothService` instantiation + event subscription, `TransferModeComboBox_SelectionChanged`, `BtScanButton_Click` (uses `BluetoothService.FindDevicesAsync()`), `PairBluetoothDevice()` (triggers system pairing via `ConnectAsync`), `ConnectToPairedDevice()` (via `ConnectByAddressAsync`), `DisconnectBluetooth()`, `ForgetBluetoothDevice()`, `RenderPairedDevices()` / `RenderDiscoveredBtDevices()` dynamic UI, Bluetooth event handlers updating connection status
  - On `SessionReady`, device auto-added to paired devices list with Bluetooth address persistence
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ConfigStore.cs ŌĆö TransferMode, PairedBluetoothDevice, BluetoothConnectionStatus, paired device management
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml ŌĆö Bluetooth UI section
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö Bluetooth event wiring, scan/pair/connect/disconnect/forget logic
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Windows Bluetooth UI patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-046 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `DeviceInformationCollection` implements `IEnumerable<DeviceInformation>` ŌĆö use `.ToList()` with `System.Linq` for mutable lists.
  - `BluetoothDevice.BluetoothAddress` is a `ulong` ŌĆö format to MAC address string via `BitConverter.GetBytes()` (6 bytes, little-endian order means bytes[5] is the MSB).
  - WPF `ComboBox` `Tag` property is a convenient way to associate enum values with items without a full converter.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö Windows-only changes require manual verification on a Windows host.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-047
- What was implemented:
  - `SendService` now accepts `BluetoothService` dependency and routes sends based on `ConfigStore.TransferMode`
  - Bluetooth text send: dispatches to `BluetoothSession.SendTextAsync()` with `IProgress<double>` callback
  - Bluetooth file send: dispatches to `BluetoothSession.SendFileAsync()` with chunk-streaming and progress
  - `SendService.BluetoothSendProgress` event drives the existing `UploadProgressPanel` in MainWindow
  - `ConfigStore.IsSendReady` property: returns true for LAN mode when configured, Bluetooth mode when connected
  - All send entry points (Ctrl+V, drag-drop in MainWindow, FloatingBall drop) route through `SendService`
  - FloatingBall readiness check updated to show mode-appropriate error message
  - Toast notifications on success/failure for both text and file Bluetooth sends
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/SendService.cs ŌĆö added BluetoothService dep, Bluetooth send paths, BluetoothSendProgress event
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/ConfigStore.cs ŌĆö added IsSendReady property
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/App.xaml.cs ŌĆö pass BluetoothService to SendService constructor
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö expose BluetoothService, SendService setter wires progress event, OnBluetoothSendProgress handler, IsSendReady guards
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/FloatingBallWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö IsSendReady guard with mode-appropriate error
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented send routing and progress patterns
  - prd.json ŌĆö US-047 passes: true
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `SendService` is the single send dispatcher for all UI entry points ŌĆö both LAN and Bluetooth. Never bypass it for sends.
  - `IsSendReady` on `ConfigStore` is the universal readiness check ŌĆö use it instead of `IsConfigured` in send guards.
  - `BluetoothSendProgress` event uses `Action<double>` (not `IProgress<double>`) since it fires from `Progress<T>` callbacks that may arrive on the thread pool ŌĆö the MainWindow handler marshals to the dispatcher.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö Windows-only changes require manual verification on a Windows host.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-048
- What was implemented:
  - BluetoothService.cs: Added `TransferReceived`, `ReceiveProgress`, `ReceiveFailed` events, wired from session in `CreateSession()`
  - MainWindow.xaml: Added `BtReceiveProgressPanel` (ProgressBar + TextBlock) in Bluetooth section
  - MainWindow.xaml.cs: Subscribed to new BluetoothService events in constructor
  - `OnBluetoothTransferReceived`: Text ŌåÆ `Clipboard.SetText()` + toast. File ŌåÆ save to ~/Downloads with duplicate-name avoidance + byte count verification + toast
  - `OnBluetoothReceiveProgress`: Drives BtReceiveProgressPanel with filename and percentage
  - `OnBluetoothReceiveFailed`: Hides progress panel + error toast
  - `StartBluetoothServerIfNeeded()`: Starts RFCOMM server in Bluetooth mode (mirrors macOS `startBluetoothServerIfNeeded`)
  - Called from `InitializeTransferModeUI()` (launch) and `TransferModeComboBox_SelectionChanged` (mode switch)
- Files changed:
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/BluetoothService.cs ŌĆö 3 new events + session event forwarding
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml ŌĆö BtReceiveProgressPanel
  - windows/CopyEverywhere/MainWindow.xaml.cs ŌĆö event handlers, RFCOMM server auto-start
  - CLAUDE.md ŌĆö documented Bluetooth receive and RFCOMM server auto-start patterns
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `BluetoothSession` already had the complete receive loop and events ŌĆö only service-level forwarding and UI wiring were missing.
  - `BluetoothService.CreateSession()` is the single place to wire all session events ŌĆö don't wire them elsewhere.
  - RFCOMM server must be explicitly started for the device to accept inbound Bluetooth connections ŌĆö it's not automatic.
  - File save to Downloads needs duplicate-name avoidance (append counter) since Bluetooth receive doesn't have clip IDs.
  - `dotnet build` cannot be verified on macOS dev machine ŌĆö Windows-only changes require manual verification on a Windows host.
---

## 2026-04-12 - US-049
- What was implemented:
  - ConfigStore.setTransferMode() now stops SSE when switching to Bluetooth and restarts SSE + queue fetch when switching back to LAN
  - MainPanelView conditionally shows server queue (LAN mode) or Bluetooth status section (BT mode)
  - Bluetooth status section shows connection status badge (color + text), connected device name, and guidance when not paired
  - Queue polling timer only starts when in LAN mode
  - Helper functions bluetoothStatusColor/bluetoothStatusText for BT connection status display
- Files changed:
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/ConfigStore.swift ŌĆö setTransferMode stops/starts SSE and queue fetch on mode switch
  - macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/MainPanelView.swift ŌĆö conditional queue/BT status sections, BT status helpers, LAN-only queue timer
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Most of US-049 was already implemented in earlier stories (picker, LAN/BT config sections, mode persistence). The gaps were service lifecycle on switch and panel mode awareness.
  - BluetoothConnectionStatus enum cases: .disconnected, .connecting, .connected, .error(String). Use switch exhaustively for UI color/text mapping.
  - `bluetoothConnectedDeviceName` on ConfigStore is the canonical way to show which BT device is connected (not BluetoothService.activeSession).
---
